name: Nifty 50
summary: WEEKEND GAP RISK: Nifty 22,713 flat (+0.15% Apr 3). Markets closed Apr 4-5. Monday absorbs F-15 loss + April 6 deadline + Kuwait/Bahrain strikes. Bank Nifty -14.8% 1M worst sector. FPIs Rs 19,837 cr out in 2 April sessions. 200-DMA at 22,000 — 1% buffer. RBI MPC April 6-8.

current.snapshot_note: April 2 close per yfinance. Trump speech fallout reversed April 1 recovery. 200-DMA at 22,000 — only 1% buffer.

price_snapshots.delta_context: Already down 5.2% YTD before full war impact

price_snapshots.trigger: Asia selloff on Trump power plant threats + FPI selling
price_snapshots.delta_context: Lowest since April 2025. ₹14T market cap wiped.

price_snapshots.trigger: Technical bounce from oversold levels, DII buying
price_snapshots.delta_context: Recovered +399 points (+1.8%). Still down 10.4% from one month ago.

price_snapshots.trigger: FY26's last trading day — worst close
price_snapshots.delta_context: Deepened from morning -0.9% to -2.14% as oil surged back to $115+ and rupee NOP rally reversed. MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%.

signals.content: Nifty futures dropped 1.7% on first trading day after strikes. Markets declined ~4% within two days. Goldman warned India 'most impacted in Asia.'
FIIs net sold ~₹52,704 crore in March. Single-day outflow ₹10,717 crore (largest of 2026). Societe Generale expects India underperformance to deepen. DII/SIP flows partially offsetting.

Nifty bounced +1.8% to 22,912 — technical recovery from April 2025 lows reached yesterday. DII buying provided support. But the bounce masks underlying weakness: FPIs sold ₹82,700 crore in March (largest monthly outflow since Oct 2024), India's PMI slowed to lowest since Oct 2022, and IPO market remains frozen. Total Indian investor losses since war began: ₹48 lakh crore (~$511 billion). The 'second only to Covid' framing in Indian media (Business Today) signals depth of concern.

Nifty fell 2.1% to 22,820 on Houthi war entry day. Sensex -2.2% to 73,583. FPIs pulled Rs 88,180 crore ($11.5B) in March. Critical support at 22,500 (200-day EMA). Bernstein warns Nifty below 20,000 if conflict persists through 2026.

Monday open: -0.9% to 22,612 despite rupee rally. Market seeing through mechanical NOP unwinding — oil at $107 still elevated, FPI selling continues. Nifty decoupled from rupee in this regime: both need OIL to fall, not just regulatory currency fix.

EVENING UPDATE: Nifty crashed from morning's 22,612 (-0.9%) to close at 22,331.40 (-2.14%, -488 points). Sensex settled at 71,947.55 (-2.22%, -1,635 points). This was FY26's LAST TRADING DAY — fiscal year ended on its worst note. Broader markets worse: MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%. Worst sectors: PSU Banks, Bank Nifty, Financial Services. Top losers: Bajaj Finance, Shriram Finance, SBI. FPI outflows hit record ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March. The oil reversal ($107→$115) and rupee collapse (93.56→95.22) during Indian trading hours drove the deepening selloff. India entered FY26 as the world's fastest-growing major economy; it ended the fiscal year with record FPI outflows, record-low rupee, and Nifty -13.9% from peak. (Sources: Business Standard, Liquide, 5paisa, NSDL)

Nifty closed at 22,331 (-2.1%) — lowest since April 2025. FY26 ended as worst fiscal year since COVID. FPI outflows ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March (RECORD). MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%. Bank Nifty -3.8% (worst sector) on NOP cap blowback. FY26 ended -13.9% from peak. Split: ~70% crisis-driven (reversible), ~30% structural (earnings slowdown). Key level: 22,000 (200-day EMA). GIFT Nifty recovered from -1.84% to +0.16% for April 1 opening. Bernstein four scenarios: 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). CONFIRMED.

FY27 Day 1 (April 1): Nifty closed at 22,679.40 (+1.6%) — India joined global relief rally but massively underperformed vs KOSPI +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%, S&P +2.9%. Brent crash -14% to $101.76 provided partial relief for import bill. Bank Nifty +2.3% recovering from March 30's -3.8% NOP carnage. Three-layer structural problem persists: (1) Crisis-reversible ~65%: oil at $101.76 still above $100, FPI record outflows ₹1.14 lakh crore in March, INR at Rs93.43. (2) Structural ~25%: earnings slowdown, record FY27 borrowing ₹17.2T, HDFC Bank NIM compression. (3) Regulatory ~10%: NOP cap forcing $30-40B unwinding (deadline April 10), STT on futures raised 0.02%→0.05%, options 0.10%→0.15%. India 10Y breached 7% (first since July 2024). India VIX at 27.89. 200-day EMA at 22,000 — 3.1% below. Bernstein scenarios: 27,500 (de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). Even with ceasefire + $85 oil, recovers only ~65% of losses. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, Bloomberg, NSDL, TradingEconomics, yfinance)

REPORTED (single source): Nifty at 23,306 (+1.72%) on April 2 per TradingEconomics — another strong rally day if confirmed. Combined with April 1's +1.6%, Sensex has gained ~2,900 points from the lows. Indian media tone shifted from "anxious" to "cautiously optimistic" on Brent crash to $101 and peace signals. (Sources: TradingEconomics)

India UNDERPERFORMED major markets — Nifty +1.6% vs KOSPI +8.44%, S&P +2.9% on April 1. Three-layer damage that de-escalation cannot fully fix: crisis-reversible (~65%), structural (~25%), regulatory (~10%). 200-day EMA at 22,000 = critical support (3.1% below). Bernstein range: 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). (Sources: multiple market data)

Nifty fell -2.0% to 22,217 on April 2 as Trump's primetime address ('hit Iran extremely hard') reversed the global peace rally. Bank Nifty worse at -2.7% to 50,080. The April 1 recovery (+1.56% close) was a false dawn — India gave back the gains and then some. Oil at $107+ keeps import bill pressure intense. FPI selling continues (₹8,331 cr net sold on April 1 alone). April 6-8 RBI MPC is the next domestic catalyst. (Sources: yfinance, Business Standard, BusinessToday)

Nifty closed April 2 at 22,713 — weekly -2.5% (-593 points). Sensex 73,319 (-2.6%, -1,953 pts). Markets CLOSED April 3 for Good Friday. FPIs sold ₹9,931 crore on April 2 alone — ACCELERATION from ₹8,331 crore on April 1. Total 2026 FPI outflows now ~₹1.31 lakh crore. 200-DMA at 22,000 remains critical — 3.2% buffer from April 2 close. RBI MPC April 6-8 is next catalyst. Oil at $109-112 devastating for import bill. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, NSDL)

Nifty 22,713 (+0.15% on April 3) — flat session, range 22,183-22,782. Markets CLOSED April 4 (Good Friday) and April 5 (Saturday). Bank Nifty -14.8% 1M — worst Indian sector by far. FPIs pulled Rs 19,837 crore in first two April sessions (Rs 8,331 cr Apr 1 + Rs 9,931 cr Apr 2 — ACCELERATING). 200-DMA at 22,000 remains critical — only 1% buffer (3.2% from April 2 close). Monday April 7 absorbs weekend developments in single session: F-15 loss, Kuwait/Bahrain strikes, April 6 energy pause deadline outcome. RBI MPC starts April 6 (decision April 8). Gap risk is extreme — all weekend news priced in one open. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, NSDL, yfinance)

edges.context: Primary equity benchmark. India underperforming global rally — Nifty +1.6% vs KOSPI +8.44%, S&P +2.9%. Three-layer problem persists. Bernstein range: 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis).
Weak rupee → FIIs sell → Nifty falls → more rupee pressure. INR improving to 92.64 from 93.19 on Brent crash. NOP cap deadline April 10 creating mechanical INR buying pressure.
Oil spike → India macro deterioration → earnings downgrades → Nifty weakness. Brent crash to $101.97 easing pressure. ~65% of Nifty decline is crisis-driven (oil-dependent, reversible).
RBI MPC April 6-8. Rate cuts needed for valuation support but RBI trapped by oil inflation. NOP cap deadline April 10. India 10Y at 7.00-7.04%. Bank Nifty recovering from NOP carnage.

trigger_points.mechanism: Psychological level. Would trigger more panic selling, margin calls, and potential capital controls discussion.

200-day EMA break triggers systematic/algo selling. Margin calls cascade. FPI selling accelerates. Currently 3.1% above at 22,679. This is the technical line that separates correction from bear market territory.
